session_name: Dexcom G7 J-PAKE Key Confirmation Vectors
description: Test vectors for J-PAKE key confirmation phase: shared key derivation and mutual authentication. After Round 2, both parties compute the same shared key and exchange confirmation hashes to prove they derived the same value.

protocol_overview.name: J-PAKE Key Confirmation (RFC 8236)
protocol_overview.purpose: Verify both parties derived the same shared key without revealing it
protocol_overview.prerequisite: Round 2 complete - both parties have A and B values

key_confirmation_format.description: Key confirmation exchanges confirmation hashes to prove shared key derivation

confirmHash.description: Confirmation hash proving shared key knowledge

shared_key_derivation.description: Shared key computation after Round 2 (per xDrip implementation)

steps.description: Compute exponent: x2s = x2 * s mod n
Compute subtraction point: subtract = g^x4 * x2s (point multiplication)
Truncate to 16 bytes for session key

shared_key_derivation.security_note: Both parties compute the same K because: K_client = K_sensor (J-PAKE correctness property)

confirmation_hash_computation.description: Confirmation hash proves knowledge of shared key without revealing it

steps.description: Double the challenge: input = challenge || challenge (16 bytes)
AES-128 encrypt with session key: output = AES(session_key, input)

our_variant.name: Hash-based confirmation (simplified)
our_variant.note: Simplified implementation - xDrip uses AES-based confirmation

test_vectors.name: Confirmation message structure validation
test_vectors.description: Verify confirmation message has correct size and opcode

test_vectors.name: Session key length
test_vectors.description: Derived session key is 16 bytes (AES-128 compatible)
test_vectors.note: Some implementations use 32-byte key, truncation happens at usage

test_vectors.name: Confirmation hash determinism
test_vectors.description: Same key + role produces same confirmation hash

test_vectors.name: Initiator vs responder hashes differ
test_vectors.description: Different roles produce different confirmation hashes

test_vectors.name: State transition to authenticated
test_vectors.description: Successful confirmation transitions to authenticated state

test_vectors.name: Failed confirmation stays failed
test_vectors.description: Wrong confirmation hash causes authentication failure

test_vectors.name: Session key is non-zero
test_vectors.description: Derived session key should not be all zeros

test_vectors.name: Confirmation rejects wrong state
test_vectors.description: Confirmation from wrong state throws error

test_vectors.name: Confirmation hash varies with key
test_vectors.description: Different session keys produce different confirmation hashes

xdrip_compatibility.description: Compatibility notes with xDrip libkeks key confirmation

wire_format_examples.name: Key Confirm RX (sensor to client)
wire_format_examples.description: Sensor responds with its confirmation hash

security_properties.key_confirmation: Proves both parties have same key
security_properties.no_key_reveal: Confirmation hash doesn't expose session key
